data.epi_content:
I approached her quietly as she seemed lost in thought and whispered softly in her ear.

"I went too far. It was something only I experienced, and you wouldn't even remember it. I just got angry and shouted, forcing you to empathize. I'm sorry, Vivian. Ha."

I gently embraced Vivian.

"But I died so many times, truly so many times. It sounds impossible, doesn't it? A person dying and then time rewinding, and it happening over and over. Right? How could you possibly know what I went through?"

As Vivian began to sob slightly, I patted her back comfortingly, and she started crying with small trembles.

"You did nothing wrong. It's just that I've become a bit strange. I died a few times, so I should just endure it. Why did I suddenly go crazy and start killing innocent people? I'm just a useless woman, pathetic and doll-sized, good for nothing but being carried around."

I pushed Vivian away.

She looked at me with suddenly trembling eyes.

"But Evan came looking for me. Through time. Even when I died, he didn't abandon me. In the end, when I was blinded with all my limbs cut off, just as you said, it wasn't you who came to save me—it was Evan. It was Evan! You pretend to care about me all the time, even imprisoned me so I couldn't die, but after I died, it wasn't you who came looking—it was Evan. You're a damn liar and a horrible, cruel person."

"I didn't do anything..."

I ignored Vivian's attempt to explain.

I wasn't trying to have a conversation—just unleashing a one-sided tirade.

"That's why you've never felt an emotion like love your whole life, because you're a madwoman with something broken inside you. Maybe you're marrying a man because everyone else gets married and has children around this age. So everyone will think you're normal! When in reality, you're a mentally ill person who knows nothing about emotions, ha!"

At those words, Vivian finally shed tears.

Realizing she had almost fallen for my words, she straightened her posture and pointed her staff at me defensively.

"You were probably having a grand time under the Emperor while I became a punching bag, having my insides chewed up. You never once helped or saved me when it really mattered. Unless you were the one who killed me. In that sense, I think I'm quite gentlemanly. I don't express hatred toward you without reason or condition."

She flinches slightly whenever I say such things, but it seems she's trying not to pay much attention now.

"Then what should I have done? Dead people are dead. Evan was the crazy one for trying to somehow save someone who was already dead!"

"You're always right. That's the correct view. Of course Evan was crazy. People shouldn't kill, shouldn't do bad things, and if sacrificing one friend can save many strangers, that's what should be done. But you never sacrifice anything, because the world made you that way. And Evan sacrificed himself to save me."

She makes excuses that there was nothing she could do, that she just abandoned me. That would be natural, and I would have done the same if Vivian had died, but since it didn't happen to me, I condemn Vivian without any pangs of conscience.
data.epi_content2:
"So shut up, Vivian. You abandoned me, and Evan tried to save me somehow. When you were slowly torturing me to death, and when I finally escaped you and was burning to death, only Evan was kneeling before me, apologizing."

I show her exactly what I saw then. Evan kneeling, apologizing, while Vivian just mechanically sprayed water.

"Realistically and naturally, what else could you have done besides spraying water? You are common sense, you are the standard, you're always right. But the world isn't a place that runs on rationality and reason."

Vivian slightly raised her lips to make an excuse but closed her mouth again. Seems she has nothing to say.

"It's somewhat diluted now, but I still hate you. My family was ruined because of you. My brother was beheaded because of you. My father hanged himself because of you. Don't say it was the Emperor—it was all because of you. Because of you, damn it. I hadn't done anything wrong, never had blood on my hands my entire life, and was living happily. Then you appeared, took Evan away, took away my dignity, reduced me to a worthless cripple, and after several deaths, you even killed me yourself. You should kneel before me like Evan and beg. Say you're sorry, say you were wrong. You vile, dog-like woman. You lock yourself in your perfect happy world and try to impose it on others, you lunatic. What's so terrible about one child dying, one woman dying, one old person dying? Because of you dragging away all the decent men from the village, there are hardly any normal people left. Deserters, thieves, robbers, low-ranking nobles who rule like kings after escaping the army, noble ladies who handle multiple men and grind beautiful women's faces into the ground, crazy old people who prefer to eat children because adult meat is tough—it's right to make excuses, but there are hardly any normal humans left. You know that these idiotic behaviors, like skeletons continuing to guard the walls after dying, are phenomena you created. You stupidly foolish woman."

Vivian trembled, her legs shaking slightly, and she grabbed her thighs, yet still lectured me about morality.

"Even so, do you think it makes sense for two people to directly kill hundreds of thousands? Including innocent elderly, children, and even pregnant women!?"

"Yes. Of course."

At those words, Vivian grimaced and gathered a spell.

As soon as it flies toward me, I'll run away immediately.

I might get hit by one spell.

It needs to be fast.

"If a million have to die for me to live, then of course that's how it should be. The most important thing in the world is ultimately me. Me. That's why when Evan came to me, whispering his proposal, I wanted it and actually did it. I told you, you abandoned me. Of course you had to. Because those who are already dead are useless."

Of course I couldn't feel nothing while killing people one by one.

I just became numb.

And Evan was far more important to me than those who died innocently and pitifully by my hand.

To me, the noble priestess trying to protect children and the men who gang up twelve to one on a woman look exactly the same—just materials.

"But you can't do anything. You never choose anything for yourself. You're just swayed by what the world considers moral. Thanks to that, you've dried one person to death. You killed me too. Vivian, according to your common sense, if something goes, something should come back. Since you killed me before, how about offering your life in return?"

Vivian pushes me away as I approach.

"Well, you probably don't want to understand. Everything I say sounds like nonsense to you. If you hear that I come back even after being killed, you might think it's better not to kill me. Most of what I say is nonsense anyway. Ha."

"...From the beginning, it doesn't make sense. With almost no magical power, how could you possibly turn back time?"
data.epi_content3:
Well, some deity who wanted to see me suffer, I could return whenever the situation was sufficiently painful.

What a fool not to listen to an experienced person—I didn't know Vivian was such a stupid child.

"Yes, logically speaking, that's true. But isn't it also illogical that the two of us are killing so many people? Two people are more important than one, and the miserable reality is more important than a piece of meat that was once close but is now dead. Of course, for you and Evan, that dead piece of meat was probably more important."

Vivian didn't answer.

She didn't say anything.

It's just absurd.

Suddenly a friend you were close with comes back in time, commits serial murders in the Academy, runs away, and goes around sacrificing people.

How could such a terrible misfortune exist in the world?

"That's why you're cursed. You try to crush me with common sense, concepts, and the imperative of morality. You're always good, and I'm always evil. So naturally I should be crushed."

For the crime of displeasing the protagonist, I should be burned to death.

Suicide is not allowed.

Since the protagonist considers it evil, those who attempt it should be locked in a room and mentally dried to death.

But I won't give in.

I've been broken many times, knelt down, and begged like a dog, but when an opportunity comes, I must seize it.

And this seems like my last chance.

If I break here, it will be harder to rise again.

"I'm sorry, I was just venting. It's been a while since I talked to a person."

I've met many things in human form, but none of them were actually people.

In contrast, Vivian is definitely human.

She's the protagonist of the world, and we can communicate somewhat.

"What I want to say is this:

It was Evan who saved me, not you, and now I'm going to save Evan.

Of course, relying on your merciful choice."

If she shakes her head or utters even slightly negative words, I'll immediately flee north this time.

There are no villages left around here these days.

Vivian is just crying.
